dry_bulk.confluence_note: A significant gap exists between record-high spot rates (99th percentile) and deteriorating macro fundamentals characterized by a 4.5% YOY drop in Chinese steel production.
dry_bulk.summary: While the BDI remains in an expansion regime with Capesize rates at multi-year highs, analyst sentiment has shifted toward a neutral/negative bias. High portside iron ore inventories (+21.9% YOY) and a projected 2H Asia slowdown suggest the current rate strength lacks a fundamental floor.
dry_bulk.outlook: Bearish tilt for the second half of 2026 as spot rates are expected to correct toward weakening demand reality.
dry_bulk.watch: Chinese industrial activity levels and potential destocking of iron ore at ports.

tanker.confluence_note: Extreme quantitative expansion, especially in clean tankers (ROC60 +127.5%), is reinforced by positive analyst sentiment and structural supply disruptions.
tanker.summary: The tanker market continues to react to geopolitical shocks that have rendered key straits unnavigable, forcing longer hauls and boosting VLCC demand. Despite high oil prices, OPEC supply growth (+7.2% YOY) and robust China oil imports (+6.1% YOY) provide strong fundamental support.
tanker.outlook: Maintained bullishness as geopolitical risk premiums and tonnage tightening outweigh near-term momentum cooling.
tanker.watch: Duration of strait blockades and OECD crude stock drawdown rates.

macro_note: The global shipping market is sharply divided; tankers are thriving on geopolitical disruption and energy demand, while dry bulk is increasingly vulnerable to a cooling Chinese industrial sector.
